content.text:
```json { "module": "financial-engine", "scores": { "visual_polish": 62, "domain_depth": 68, "data_richness": 55, "interaction_quality": 52, "ai_integration": 35, "mobile_first": 40, "performance": 58, "typescript_quality": 65, "error_handling": 55, "realtime": 48, "accessibility": 38, "security": 50, "differentiation": 45, "enterprise_readiness": 42 }, "overall_score": 51, "issues": [ { "id": "financial-engine-C4-001", "severity": "critical", "dimension": "enterprise_readiness", "title": "Pay Application page missing AIA G702/G703 PDF generation — P0 roadmap item", "file": "src/services/pdf/paymentAppPdf.ts", "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §10 requires pixel-perfect AIA G702/G703 PDF export. PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P1-1 specifies this as a deal winner. No PDF generation service exists. GCs cannot bill owners without these forms.", "prompt": "Create the new file src/services/pdf/paymentAppPdf.ts. This file does not exist yet. Create it with these exports: (1) `generateG702Pdf(payApp: PayApplication, project: { name: string; address: string; owner: string; architect: string; contractor: string }, sovLines: PayAppLineItem[]): Promise` — uses `pdf-lib` (already in package.json or install it) to generate an AIA G702 summary page. The PDF must include: project name, application number, period dates, original contract sum, net change by COs, contract sum to date, total completed and stored to date (from computeCurrentPaymentDue in payApplications.ts), retainage held, total earned less retainage, less previous certificates, current payment due, and signature blocks for contractor and architect. (2) `generateG703Pdf(sovLines: PayAppLineItem[]): Promise` — generates the continuation sheet with columns: Item #, Description, Scheduled Value, Work Completed Previous, Work Completed This Period, Materials Stored, Total Completed & Stored, % Complete, Balance to Finish, Retainage. Each line uses computeCurrentPaymentDue for calculations. Use integer cents internally, format to dollars with 2 decimal places on the PDF. Add running totals row at page bottom. BEFORE: No PDF generation exists for pay applications. AFTER: Calling generateG702Pdf returns a valid PDF Uint8Array that can be downloaded. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "financial-engine-C4-002", "severity": "critical", "dimension": "domain_depth", "title": "Lien waiver tracking has no dedicated UI page despite being a core GC workflow", "file": "src/pages/LienWaivers.tsx", "description": "PRODUCTION_ROADMAP and MODULE_SPECS list lien waiver tracking as a major workflow. The database has a lien_waivers table and autoGenerateLienWaivers exists in the API, but there is no dedicated page. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §10 criterion 10 requires lien waiver integration with pay apps.", "prompt": "Create the new file src/pages/LienWaivers.tsx. This file does not exist yet. Create a full page component that: (1) Imports theme tokens from '../styles/theme' and primitives from '../components/Primitives'. (2) Shows 4 MetricBox cards at top: Total Waivers, Pending Signature, Signed This Period, Missing Waivers (red if > 0). (3) Renders a table with columns: Vendor Name, Waiver Type (conditional_progress, unconditional_progress, conditional_final, unconditional_final — display as readable labels without hyphens), Amount Waived (formatted as dollars), Period Covered Through, Status (signed/unsigned with green/amber dots), Signed Date. (4) Each row is hoverable with cursor pointer. (5) Includes a filter bar with waiver type dropdown and status toggle (All/Pending/Signed). (6) Empty state: 'No lien waivers for this period. Waivers are generated automatically when pay applications are approved.' with a button 'View Pay Applications'. (7) Uses useEffect to fetch from supabase.from('lien_waivers').select('*').eq('project_id', activeProjectId). (8) Loading state uses Skeleton components matching the table layout. BEFORE: No lien waiver page exists in the app. AFTER: /lien-waivers route renders a fully functional lien waiver tracking page with real data from Supabase. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "financial-engine-C4-003", "severity": "high", "dimension": "accessibility", "title": "SCurve chart lacks screen reader data table fallback and keyboard navigation labels", "file": "src/components/budget/SCurve.tsx", "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require axe-core zero critical violations. The SCurve SVG chart has basic aria-label but no accessible data table fallback for screen readers, and keyboard navigation announces no values. Interactive points have no aria-labels with actual dollar amounts.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/budget/SCurve.tsx, in the SCurve component, make these specific changes: (1) After the closing tag, add a visually hidden data table (position: absolute, width: 1px, height: 1px, overflow: hidden, clip: rect(0,0,0,0)) with containing a , with columns Month, Planned ($M), Actual ($M), and rows for each month from the months array mapping to plannedData and actualData values. This gives screen readers a tabular representation. (2) In the onKeyDown handler, when hovered changes, add an aria-live='polite' region div (already visually hidden) that announces: `Month: ${months[hovered]}, Planned: $${plannedData[hovered]}M, Actual: $${actualData[hovered] || 'N/A'}M`. Create a state variable `announcement` and update it in the key handler. Render a
{announcement}
. (3) On each interactive circle element in the SVG, add aria-label={`${months[i]}: Actual $${actualData[i]}M`}. BEFORE: Screen readers only hear the generic SVG label, no data values. AFTER: Screen readers can access all chart data via hidden table and live announcements during keyboard navigation. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "financial-engine-C4-004", "severity": "high", "dimension": "mobile_first", "title": "WaterfallChart and Treemap do not collapse gracefully below 375px (iPhone SE)", "file": "src/components/budget/WaterfallChart.tsx", "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require usability at 375px width with no horizontal scroll. The WaterfallChart mobile breakpoint is 768px but at 375px the 90px label column plus bar track causes overflow. The label text truncates poorly and touch targets on bars are under 44px.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/budget/WaterfallChart.tsx, in the WaterfallChart component, make these specific changes: (1) Change the mobile breakpoint detection from `window.innerWidth < 768` to `window.innerWidth < 640` for the existing isMobile state, and add a new state `isNarrow` initialized as `window.innerWidth < 400`. Update the resize handler to set both. (2) When isNarrow is true, change the mobile horizontal bar layout: reduce the label width from 90 to 64, set fontSize to typography.fontSize.xs (instead of sm), and abbreviate labels: 'Original Contract' to 'Original', 'Approved COs' to 'Approved', 'Pending COs' to 'Pending', 'Revised Contract' to 'Revised'. (3) Set minimum bar height to 44 (matching Apple HIG touch target) by changing minHeight from 56 to 44 on the bar container div, but ensure the inner bar div has minHeight: 44 as well. (4) Add overflow: 'hidden' and textOverflow: 'ellipsis' and whiteSpace: 'nowrap' to the label span to prevent text wrapping that causes layout shift. BEFORE: At 375px width, bars overflow or compress to unreadable sizes. AFTER: Chart renders cleanly at 375px with readable abbreviated labels and 44px touch targets. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "financial-engine-C4-005", "severity": "high", "dimension": "realtime", "title": "Budget page does not subscribe to pay_applications or lien_waivers table changes", "file": "src/components/budget/EarnedValueDashboard.tsx", "description": "The EarnedValueDashboard subscribes to budget_items and change_orders but not to payment_applications or subcontractor_invoices. When a pay app is approved or an invoice is recorded, the Earned Value metrics (ACWP, CPI) do not update until manual refresh. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §5 criterion 11 requires real-time budget updates.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/budget/EarnedValueDashboard.tsx, in the EarnedValueDashboard component, find the useEffect that creates the Supabase channel 'ev-dashboard-' + activeProjectId. BEFORE: The channel subscribes to only two tables: budget_items and change_orders. AFTER: Add two more .on() calls to the same channel chain: (1) `.on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'payment_applications', filter: 'project_id=eq.' + activeProjectId }, () => { refetch(); })` and (2) `.on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'subcontractor_invoices', filter: 'project_id=eq.' + activeProjectId }, () => { refetch(); })`. This ensures that when a payment application status changes (e.g. approved) or a new subcontractor invoice is recorded, the EV dashboard automatically recalculates CPI, SPI, EAC, and other metrics without requiring a page refresh. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." } ], "invented_features": [ { "title": "13 Week Cash Flow Projection Engine", "description": "Build a forward-looking cash flow chart that projects 13 weeks of inflows (from approved pay apps and collection timing) against outflows (sub payments, material POs, payroll) using committed cost data, retainage schedules, and historical payment velocity. Render as a line chart with net cash position, cumulative inflows, and cumulative outflows. Flag weeks where projected cash goes negative. This is the CFO feature that Procore cannot do natively and is explicitly called out in the Product Vision success metrics: 'A CFO can see the 13 week cash flow projection for any project in 2 clicks.'" } ], "competitive_intel": "Procore's financial module requires manual data entry for pay apps and has no native G702/G703 PDF generation — users export to Excel and fill AIA forms manually. Procore has no lien waiver workflow tied to pay app approval. SiteSync's computeCurrentPaymentDue function with proper AIA line item math (5, 5a, 5b, 6, 7, 8) is a genuine differentiator, but without the PDF output it is invisible to end users. Sage Intacct integration (P0-1 in roadmap) would leapfrog Procore's financial depth overnight since 70%+ of target GCs use Sage. Fieldwire and Buildertrend have zero financial capabilities.", "summary": "The Financial Engine has strong domain logic foundations — the computeCurrentPaymentDue function correctly implements AIA G702 math with integer cent rounding, and the EarnedValueDashboard computes CPI/SPI/EAC properly. However, two critical gaps prevent this from being shippable: (1) no AIA G702/G703 PDF generation means PMs cannot actually produce the billing documents that drive cash flow, and (2) no lien waiver tracking page means GCs cannot manage the compliance documents that gate every payment. The Earned Value dashboard is a genuine differentiator but its real-time subscriptions are incomplete (missing pay app and invoice tables), and the charts fail accessibility standards with no screen reader data fallback. Mobile rendering breaks below 400px on the waterfall and treemap charts. The five issues above target: PDF generation (the highest-impact P1 roadmap item), lien waiver UI (missing P0 workflow), accessibility compliance, mobile responsiveness, and real-time completeness." } ```
S Curve planned versus actual spend
